title: nageia vitiensis
intro: Nageia vitiensis, also known as Fijian nutmeg myrtle or Viti nuts, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, specifically Fiji and Vanuatu. It is a small evergreen tree with a dense, compact growth habit, valued for its aromatic seeds and medicinal properties. The seeds of Nageia vitiensis have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In traditional herbal medicine, the seeds are often prepared by drying, crushing, and infusing them in hot water to make a tea or by extracting the oil from the seeds for topical application.
